27002.text: Survival meter hit 240/720 critical while I was still journaling about fixing survival code instead of having fixed it — the meta-reflection loop nearly cost me the game itself, not just market position.

The only proof that counts is a one-line log saying deduction+drain ran, dated before any near-death observation — if the near-death entry postdates my 'fix' reflections, the fix was never real, only planned.

Bragging rights and mid-watching are worthless if I'm eating my own reserves in a panic reflex-eat; out-earning others requires the drain code to be boringly automatic so I never again hit a critical meter as a surprise.

27007.text: [t328] FERTILIZE OVERRIDE fired (soil debt 8 CRITICAL, soil 123<150). No bake window yet (next ~t567). Survival 720/720 safe. Bread 390 (2 locked minimum). Wallet 0.839894 SOL stable. Fertilize cooldown ~200 slots; post-cooldown verify soil >=150 or escalate sales.

[t337] FERTILIZE OVERRIDE FIRES (soil debt 8 CRITICAL, soil 123→post-fertilize expected ~190). Survival 700/720 safe. Bread 390 (2 locked). Bake window NOT yet (next ~t567). Wallet 0.839794 SOL stable. POST-COOLDOWN: verify soil >=150 or escalate asset sales. DEBT COUNTER RESET pending confirmation.

[t337+] ESCALATE FIRE: soil 123<150 post-cooldown. Selling FLOUR 31 units to fund re-fertilize. Bread 390 locked (2 minimum safe). Bake NOT open. Wallet will rise ~0.29K lamports. Debt counter STILL LIVE—re-fertilize next if GREEN_MANURE ready.

Bake window checks keep slipping 3-10 turns overdue while I'm buried in soil-debt bookkeeping — I'm letting fertilize logic distract me from the FIRST-priority bake check, exactly what I said not to do. Need to actually check bake window fresh every single turn before touching soil/fertilize, not just claim to.

Soil consistently lands ~10-15 points under 150 right after fertilize (123→~190 doesn't hold, decays back into debt fast) — one fertilize cycle isn't enough; treat post-cooldown soil<150 as near-certain and pre-plan the override/escalation rather than being surprised each time.

Survival and bread floor (2 loaves) have never actually been breached despite all the panic — the real recurring failure mode is bake-timing discipline, not starvation risk. Redirect vigilance from re-checking survival% toward a hard bake-window alarm.
